'''Obitoki''', a [[Alien/Legends|non-Human]] [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|Podracer]], [[Podracing/Legends|competed]] against a [[Dug/Legends|Dug]] named [[Sebulba/Legends|Sebulba]] sometime before [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]]. During the race, Sebulba disabled Obitoki's [[Podracer/Legends|pod]] by shooting flames into one of its [[Engine/Legends|engines]] after Obitoki tried to overtake him.

==Biography==
Obitoki was a [[Alien/Legends|non-Human]] [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|Podracer]] active in the [[Time/Legends|time]] frame<ref name="CSWE" /> preceding [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]].<ref name="JQTDG">As of the events of the novel ''[[Jedi Quest: The Dangerous Games]]'', which takes place in [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]], [[Sebulba/Legends|Sebulba]] had retired from [[Podracing/Legends|Podracing]] to coach his son, [[Hekula]].</ref> In one [[Podracing/Legends|race]] against the [[Dug/Legends|Dug]] [[Sebulba/Legends|Sebulba]], Obitoki tried to overtake the competitor, who responded by opening a side vent on his [[Podracer/Legends|pod]] and spewing fire into Obitoki's [[Engine/Legends|engine]]. Obitoki's pod exploded and crashed while expelling a cloud of debris, removing Obitoki from the race. Another racer, [[Habba Kee]], tried to navigate the wreckage, but he collided with Obitoki's pod, knocking him out of the race as well.<ref name="CSWE" />

==Behind the scenes==
Obitoki appeared in the [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' novelization]], written by [[Terry Brooks]] and published just before the release of the [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|film]] in [[1999]]. The character was also depicted in another tie-in entitled [[Star Wars Journal: Anakin Skywalker|''Star Wars Episode I Journal: Anakin Skywalker'']]. In the novels, Obitoki and Habba Kee [[Death/Legends|die]] in the crash described above during the [[Boonta Eve Classic (32 BBY)/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]] of [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. In the same context, Obitoki and Kee are mentioned in the movie's script,<ref name="Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace Illustrated Screenplay">[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace Illustrated Screenplay|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace Illustrated Screenplay'']]</ref> but neither makes an appearance in the film itself. While the novel has racers [[Kam Nale|Elan Mak]], Kee, and Obitoki surpassing another driver named [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] during the race's second lap, the trio is replaced by [[Ark Roose/Legends|Ark Roose]], [[Gasgano/Legends|Gasgano]], and [[Teemto Pagalies/Legends|Teemto Pagalies]] in the movie, none of whom crash in the way Obitoki does. Additionally, the reference book ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Incredible Cross-Sections]]'' establishes that eighteen racers qualified and competed in the [[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]], but Obitoki is not on the provided list. This article thereby treats the appearance in the novel as ambiguously [[canon]]ical within the ''[[Star Wars Legends]]'' continuity. However, ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''{{'s}} entry on Obitoki confirms that the racer existed and crashed in the way described, but it leaves the wreck's occurrence during the Boonta and Obitoki's death ambiguous.
